resources.string: If turned on, it shows your reference type and value as a label (e.g. Limit: 10) on the card
If turned on, it shows the highest amount ever logged for this activity as a label on the card.
Deleting this activity will also delete all progress you made. Unless you backed-up your data recently, you won\'t be able to undo this.
Deleting this check-in cannot be undone.
Failed to create the backup file
Import a backup file into Damier.
No activities yet. Add one to get started.
When enabled, crash reports and error logs are sent to Firebase to help improve the app
App needs to restart to apply changes
Learn how your data is handled.
Total daily amounts for the current week in comparison to last week
The chip was removed too early. Please try again.
This chip is not compatible with Cue.
This chip is write-protected and cannot be used
This chip is too small to store the required data.
Whether you want to do more, reach a target, or hold yourself back, Damier keeps it all in one board, one glance away.
Set a daily target. The cell-color gets stronger as you get closer, being fully colored once you reach it.
The cell grows redder as you approach your limit. At the limit, a ! marks that you\'ve hit it.
Log as much as you can each day. The cell color gets stronger as your value grows.
The more progress, the stronger the color
There are two ways to log an activity. Both count, they just give you different levels of control.
Tap the bolt on the board or home screen widget. This logs the default amount instantly
The default amount is set per activity, you can configure it individually when creating or editing an activity.
Available on: board, home screen widget
Tap the bolt on the detail page. Opens a dialog so you can enter the exact amount.
That\'s everything you need to know. The rest you\'ll figure out along the way.
Your personal best across all days automatically becomes the reference.
This backup was created with an older version of Damier and is no longer supported. I\'m sorry for the inconvenience, future backups will always remain compatible.
A small group of 4–6 testers gets early access to Damier for the first time.
A second wave of 15 testers joins to help shape Damier before its public launch.
Damier is now publicly available on Google Play. Thank you for being here from the start.

string-array.name: v0_1_1_notes
string-array.item: Fixes several bugs reported after the initial release.

string-array.name: v0_2_0_notes
string-array.item: Damier is now fully available in German.
Checking in through the details page now opens a dedicated dialog for a cleaner experience.

string-array.name: v0_2_1_notes
string-array.item: Fixes the check-in dialog not following the app\'s theme correctly.

string-array.name: v0_3_0_notes
string-array.item: Statistics are here — get a better picture of your habits over time
A graph shows your total check-ins over time at a glance.
A second graph lets you compare this week\'s check-ins with last week\'s

string-array.name: v0_3_1_notes
string-array.item: Fixes the comparison chart\'s text color not adapting correctly in dark mode.
The check-in history now has a proper title.

string-array.name: v0_3_2_a_0_3_3_notes
string-array.item: Fixes a data integrity issue in the database that could cause unexpected behavior.

string-array.name: v0_4_1_notes
string-array.item: You can now link NFC chips to activities and check in with a single tap.
Fixes a database migration issue that was causing the app to crash on launch.

string-array.name: v0_4_2_notes
string-array.item: Fixes another database migration issue that could cause crashes on launch.

string-array.name: v0_4_3_notes
string-array.item: Damier now supports Android 8 (Oreo) and above, reaching more devices.
A dedicated check-in button now appears on the details page on smaller devices where the bottom bar is hidden.
The statistics sheet is now scrollable.
A cancel button has been added to the NFC dialog.
Widgets now reload correctly after a device reboot.

string-array.name: v0_5_2_notes
string-array.item: A range of visual and usability improvements across the app.
The waffle diagram now includes a legend.
The streak card has a refreshed design.
NFC tags now store the activity\'s ID instead of its name, making it possible to assign multiple tags to the same activity.

string-array.name: v0_6_0_notes
string-array.item: New users are now welcomed with an on-boarding flow that guides them through Damier\'s key features.

string-array.name: v0_6_1_notes
string-array.item: The layout for adding and editing activities has been redesigned for a cleaner look.

string-array.name: v0_7_0_notes
string-array.item: Menus and forms now open as bottom sheets for a more natural feel on Android.
Adding multiple check-ins at once now uses a stepper instead of a text field.
NFC write feedback is more responsive and the dialog closes automatically once writing is complete.

string-array.name: v0_7_1_notes
string-array.item: Fixes the date and time picker not following the app\'s theme.
The home screen widget now shows a loading indicator while the activity data is being fetched.

string-array.name: v1_0_2_notes
string-array.item: Fixes the on-boarding not appearing on the very first launch.
Fixes a bug where newly created or edited activities would not be saved.

string-array.name: v1_0_4_notes
string-array.item: Fixes an NFC issue introduced by permission changes in Android 17.

string-array.name: v1_1_0_notes
string-array.item: The backup system has been rewritten from the ground up and is now reliable across app updates.
NFC check-in confirmation is now shown as a Snackbar.
Fixes a bug where streaks were incorrectly deleted during recalculation.
Fixes the date picker showing today as unavailable on UTC+ devices.
Fixes the home screen widget getting stuck on "Loading" after deleting an activity.
Fixes an NFC reader mode issue and a related memory leak.
Backups from v1.0.x are no longer supported. Future backups will always stay compatible.

string-array.name: v1_1_1_notes
string-array.item: General stability improvements and bug fixes.

string-array.name: v1_2_0_notes
string-array.item: The full update history is now available directly in the app, from the very first alpha all the way to today.
Damier now uses Tabler Icons throughout the app for a cleaner, more consistent look.
Your longest streak is now shown on the streak card, so your personal best is always visible.
Check-ins can now be undone via a Snackbar — no more accidental check-ins with no way back.
Activities with a limit now show a visual indicator when the limit is exceeded.
You can now change Damier\'s language independently from your system language in the app settings.

string-array.name: streak_m_ns
string-array.item: Tap. Just once.
It won\'t start itself.
The button is right there.
Zero is embarrassing.
Literally just press it
Start. Please. Just start.
Well, this is awkward.
Anytime now.
Come on.
No pressure, but also start.
Bold of you to wait.

string-array.name: streak_m_s
string-array.item: You didn\'t quit. Yet.
The streak lives. For now.
Don\'t you dare stop now!
Who are you again?
Streak intact. You\'re welcome.
Still going strong. Ish.
Don\'t blow it now!
Streak status: Surprisingly alive.
Still here? Respect.
Not bad. Keep going.
The streak is fragile. Be careful.
You\'re on a roll. Tiny roll.

string-array.name: streak_m_m
string-array.item: Still here? Genuinely surprised.
The streak is getting cocky.
One week. Don\'t ruin everything.
Okay fine, you\'re consistent. Ugh.
The app didn\'t expect this.
The streak is real, handle it with care.
You\'re doing it, against all odds.
Almost a habit. Almost.
You\'re doing suspiciously well.
Getting harder to quit now. Good.
A week in. No excuses.
Habit loading… Please wait
Almost impressive. Almost.
You\'re in too deep to stop.
Breaking now would be criminal.

string-array.name: streak_m_l
string-array.item: The habit is alive.
This is who you are now.
No going back. Sorry.
You can\'t quit. It\'d be weird.
The habit is watching you sleep.
Resistance is futile now.
You\'re kind of a big deal.
The streak is permanent. Basically.
